2026-04-23T05:52:04.0121718Z [36;1m# Generate a secure random API key (360 bits of entropy, 40+ chars)[0m
2026-04-23T05:52:04.0122229Z [36;1m# Mask immediately to prevent timing vulnerabilities[0m
2026-04-23T05:52:04.0122797Z [36;1mAPI_KEY=$(openssl rand -base64 45 | tr -d '/+=')[0m
2026-04-23T05:52:04.0123119Z [36;1mecho "::add-mask::${API_KEY}"[0m
2026-04-23T05:52:04.0123361Z [36;1m[0m

2026-04-23T05:55:48.6246221Z **Summary:** PR #58482 replaced the old post-processor pruning approach with a reachability walk (`CollectReachableTypes`) and added canonical provider selection for shared resource models — but `docs/design.md` still describes the old behavior. The issue documents both gaps with exact replacement text for a coding agent to implement.

2026-04-23T05:55:52.6378133Z 
2026-04-23T05:55:52.6378303Z ◆ I found an important part of the design doc:
2026-04-23T05:55:52.6378598Z   
2026-04-23T05:55:52.6380412Z   Line 233: "The output library bypasses the mgmt output pipeline (which would trigger mgmt-specific type initialization) and instead iterates input models and enums directly, routing each through our TypeFactory. **Only resources are marked as "types to keep" — the post-processor automatically prunes unreferenced models and enums.**"
2026-04-23T05:55:52.6381788Z   
2026-04-23T05:55:52.6382699Z   This is the old description of how filtering worked (via post-processor pruning). The PR changed this to use **reachability-based** filtering at the collection phase, before passing to the type factory. The design doc is now outdated here.
2026-04-23T05:55:52.6383692Z   
2026-04-23T05:55:52.6384226Z   Let me check what the design doc currently says in section 233 area more carefully.
2026-04-23T05:55:52.6384579Z 
2026-04-23T05:55:52.6385144Z ✓ $ sed -n '225,260p' /home/runner/work/azure-sdk-for-net/azure-sdk-for-net/eng/packages/http-client-csharp-provisioning/docs/design.md
2026-04-23T05:55:52.6385697Z 
2026-04-23T05:55:52.6386064Z ◆ I've found a clear documentation gap. The design doc's "Output Library" section (section 6) says:
2026-04-23T05:55:52.6386557Z   
2026-04-23T05:55:52.6387094Z   > "Only resources are marked as 'types to keep' — the post-processor automatically prunes unreferenced models and enums."
2026-04-23T05:55:52.6387619Z   
2026-04-23T05:55:52.6388439Z   But the PR actually changed this mechanism to use **reachability-based collection** (walking the resource models' property graph to collect only reachable models/enums), replacing the previous post-processor pruning approach.
2026-04-23T05:55:52.6389608Z   
2026-04-23T05:55:52.6389795Z   There are two gaps in the design doc:
2026-04-23T05:55:52.6390213Z   
2026-04-23T05:55:52.6390841Z   1. **Output Library section** describes the old post-processor pruning approach, which has been replaced by reachability-based collection in `CollectReachableTypes()`
2026-04-23T05:55:52.6391535Z   
2026-04-23T05:55:52.6391684Z   
2026-04-23T05:55:52.6392270Z   2. **Model creation section** doesn't mention the "shared resource model" handling (multiple resources backed by the same input model, canonical provider selection)
2026-04-23T05:55:52.6392942Z   
2026-04-23T05:55:52.6394233Z   The README.md for the provisioning generator is high-level and doesn't dive into implementation details, so it probably doesn't need changes. I should update the design doc to cover the reachability-based filtering mechanism and document how shared resource models are handled with canonical provider selection in `CreateModelCore`.
2026-04-23T05:55:52.6395941Z   
2026-04-23T05:55:52.6396160Z   I'll open an issue to track this documentation gap.
2026-04-23T05:55:52.6396396Z 
2026-04-23T05:55:52.6397036Z ✓ $ grep -n "section\|##\|###" /home/runner/work/azure-sdk-for-net/azure-sdk-for-net/eng/packages/http-client-csharp-provisioning/docs/design.md | head -40

Use this for actionable work items that need assignment, labeling, and status tracking. For reports, announcements, or status updates that don't require task tracking, use create_discussion instead. CONSTRAINTS: Maximum 1 issue(s) can be created.","inputSchema":{"additionalProperties":false,"properties":{"body":{"description":"Detailed issue description in Markdown. Do NOT repeat the title as a heading since it already appears as the issue's h1. Include context, reproduction steps, or acceptance criteria as appropriate.","type":"string"},"integrity":{"description":"Trustworthiness level of the message source (e.g., \"low\", \"medium\", \"high\").","type":"string"},"labels":{"description":"Labels to categorize the issue (e.g., 'bug', 'enhancement'). Labels must exist in the repository.","items":{"type":"string"},"type":"array"},"parent":{"description":"Parent issue number for creating sub-issues. This is the numeric ID from the GitHub URL (e.g., 42 in github.com/owner/repo/issues/42). Can also be a temporary_id (e.g., 'aw_abc123', 'aw_Test123') from a previously created issue in the same workflow run.","type":["number","string"]},"secrecy":{"description":"Confidentiality level of the message content (e.g., \"public\", \"internal\", \"private\").","type":"string"},"temporary_id":{"description":"Unique temporary identifier for referencing this issue before it's created. Format: 'aw_' followed by 3 to 12 alphanumeric characters (e.g., 'aw_abc1', 'aw_Test123'). Use '#aw_ID' in body text to reference other issues by their temporary_id; these are replaced with actual issue numbers after creation.","pattern":"^aw_[A-Za-z0-9]{3,12}$","type":"string"},"title":{"description":"Concise issue title summarizing the bug, feature, or task. The title appears as the main heading, so keep it brief and descriptive.","type":"string"}},"required":["title","body"],"type":"object"},"name":"create_issue"},{"description":"Report that a tool or capability needed to complete the task is not available, or share any information you deem important about missing functionality or limitations. Use this when you cannot accomplish what was requested because the required functionality is missing or access is restricted.","inputSchema":{"additionalProperties":false,"properties":{"alternatives":{"description":"Any workarounds, manual steps, or alternative approaches the user could take (max 256 characters).","type":"string"},"integrity":{"description":"Trustworthiness level of the message source (e.g., \"low\", \"medium\", \"high\").","type":"string"},"reason":{"description":"Explanation of why this tool is needed or what information you want to share about the limitation (max 256 characters).","type":"string"},"secrecy":{"description":"Confidentiality level of the message content (e.g., \"public\", \"internal\", \"private\").","type":"string"},"tool":{"description":"Optional: Name or description of the missing tool or capability (max 128 characters). Be specific about what functionality is needed.","type":"string"}},"required":["reason"],"type":"object"},"name":"missing_tool"},{"description":"Log a transparency message when no significant actions are needed. Use this to confirm workflow completion and provide visibility when analysis is complete but no changes or outputs are required (e.g., 'No issues found', 'All checks passed'). This ensures the workflow produces human-visible output even when no other actions are taken.","inputSchema":{"additionalProperties":false,"properties":{"integrity":{"description":"Trustworthiness level of the message source (e.g., \"low\", \"medium\", \"high\").","type":"string"},"message":{"description":"Status or completion message to log. Should explain what was analyzed and the outcome (e.g., 'Code review complete - no issues found', 'Analysis complete - all tests passing').","type":"string"},"secrecy":{"description":"Confidentiality level of the message content (e.g., \"public\", \"internal\", \"private\").","type":"string"}},"required":["message"],"type":"object"},"name":"noop"},{"description":"Report that data or information needed to complete the task is not available. Use this when you cannot accomplish what was requested because required data, context, or information is missing.","inputSchema":{"additionalProperties":false,"properties":{"alternatives":{"description":"Any workarounds, manual steps, or alternative approaches the user could take (max 256 characters).","type":"string"},"context":{"description":"Additional context about the missing data or where it should come from (max 256 characters).","type":"string"},"data_type":{"description":"Type or description of the missing data or information (max 128 characters). Be specific about what data is needed.","type":"string"},"integrity":{"description":"Trustworthiness level of the message source (e.g., \"low\", \"medium\", \"high\").","type":"string"},"reason":{"description":"Explanation of why this data is needed to complete the task (max 256 characters).","type":"string"},"secrecy":{"description":"Confidentiality level of the message content (e.g., \"public\", \"internal\", \"private\").","type":"string"}},"required":[],"type":"object"},"name":"missing_data"},{"description":"Signal that the task could not be completed due to an infrastructure or tool failure (e.g., MCP server crash, missing authentication, inaccessible repository). Use this when required tools or data are unavailable and the task cannot be meaningfully performed. This is distinct from noop (no action needed) — it indicates an active failure that prevented the task from running. The workflow framework will treat this as a failure signal even when the agent exits successfully.","inputSchema":{"additionalProperties":false,"properties":{"details":{"description":"Optional extended details or diagnostic context about the failure (max 65000 characters).","type":"string"},"reason":{"description":"A concise explanation of why the task could not be completed (max 1024 characters). Be specific about which tools or resources were unavailable.","type":"string"}},"required":["reason"],"type":"object"},"name":"report_incomplete"},{"description":"Dispatch the issue triage workflow for the newly created issue","inputSchema":{"additionalProperties":false,"properties":{},"type":"object"},"name":"dispatch_triage"}]}}

2026-04-23T05:55:52.8633843Z {"timestamp":"2026-04-23T05:53:53.47310287Z","direction":"OUT","type":"REQUEST","server_id":"github","method":"tools/call","payload":{"jsonrpc":"2.0","method":"tools/call","params":{"arguments":{"issue_number":"58482","method":"get","owner":"Azure","repo":"azure-sdk-for-net"},"name":"issue_read"}}}
2026-04-23T05:55:52.8650211Z {"timestamp":"2026-04-23T05:53:53.757199486Z","direction":"IN","type":"RESPONSE","server_id":"github","payload":{"id":1,"jsonrpc":"2.0","result":{"content":[{"text":"{\"number\":58482,\"title\":\"[Provisioning generator] Only generate models reachable from resources (#56733)\",\"body\":\"Fixes #56733.\\n\\nThis PR teaches the provisioning generator to only generate the models and enums actually used by the resources it emits, instead of emitting every input model in the library. It also fixes two related issues that surface once a resource model is shared by multiple resources.\\n\\n## Reachability-based model/enum emission\\n\\n`ProvisioningOutputLibrary` now walks `ArmProviderSchema.Resources` and recursively collects every `InputModelType` / `InputEnumType` reachable through base/derived models, properties, and array/dictionary/union element types. Only those types are passed to the type factory. This drops dead types that are not part of the public Provisioning surface — list-result envelopes (`XxxListResult`), patch wrappers, error models, etc.\\n\\nWhy this matters:\\n- Generated libraries no longer carry irrelevant types (e.g. paging envelopes that have no place in a Provisioning library).\\n- Resolves #56733: the generator used to call `CreateModel` on the resource model from inside dead list-result envelopes, which threw when the same resource model backed multiple resources.\\n\\n## Supporting fixes for shared resource models\\n\\nOnce we stop emitting dead envelopes, the remaining real cases of one input model backing multiple resources (e.g. `ContainerGroupProfile` + `ContainerGroupProfileRevision` via `Azure.ResourceManager.Legacy.LegacyOperations`) need to behave correctly:\\n\\n- **`ProvisioningResourceProvider.BuildName`** — return `ResourceMetadata.ResourceName` so the two providers get distinct file/type names instead of colliding on the input model name.\\n- **`ProvisioningTypeFactory.CreateModelCore`** — when a model is shared by multiple resource providers, prefer the canonical provider whose `ResourceMetadata.ResourceName` matches the model\\u0026#39;s own name. The mgmt `FlattenPropertyVisitor` builds `OutputFlattenPropertyMap` by calling `CreateModel` on the input model, so this routes flattened forwarding properties onto the parent resource (e.g. `Profile`/`ContainerGroupProfile`) and not onto the child revision.\\n\\n## Test\\n\\nAdded `profileRevision.tsp` to the `Provisioning-TypeSpec` test project, mirroring the `ContainerGroupProfile` + `ContainerGroupProfileRevision` pattern: a `ProxyResource` `Profile` plus a virtual sub-resource `ProfileRevision` created via `Azure.ResourceManager.Legacy.LegacyOperations` reusing the same `Profile` model, with `@flattenProperty(Profile.properties)`.\\n\\nGenerated output:\\n- `Profile.cs` and `ProfileRevision.cs` are distinct files.\\n- `Profile` carries the flattened `Description` and `SkuName` properties.\\n- `ProfileRevision` only carries `Id` and `Name` (matches the Bicep-side expectation for revisions).\\n- No dead list-result envelope types are emitted under `Models/`.\\n\\nThis unblocks the `Azure.Provisioning.ContainerInstance` migration (separate PR).\\n\",\"state\":\"closed\",\"html_url\":\"https://github.com/Azure/azure-sdk-for-net/pull/58482\",\"user\":{\"login\":\"ArcturusZhang\",\"id\":10554446,\"profile_url\":\"https://github.com/ArcturusZhang\",\"avatar_url\":\"https://avatars.githubusercontent.com/u/10554446?v=4\"},\"author_association\":\"MEMBER\",\"labels\":[\"CodeGen\",\"Provisioning\"],\"reactions\":{\"total_count\":0,\"+1\":0,\"-1\":0,\"laugh\":0,\"confused\":0,\"heart\":0,\"hooray\":0,\"rocket\":0,\"eyes\":0},\"created_at\":\"2026-04-22T05:59:21Z\",\"updated_at\":\"2026-04-23T05:50:18Z\",\"closed_at\":\"2026-04-23T05:50:13Z\",\"closed_by\":\"ArcturusZhang\"}","type":"text"}]}}}
2026-04-23T05:55:52.8664114Z {"timestamp":"2026-04-23T05:53:53.768996585Z","direction":"OUT","type":"REQUEST","server_id":"github","method":"tools/call","payload":{"jsonrpc":"2.0","method":"tools/call","params":{"arguments":{"issue_number":58482,"method":"get","owner":"Azure","repo":"azure-sdk-for-net"},"name":"issue_read"}}}
2026-04-23T05:55:52.8680164Z {"timestamp":"2026-04-23T05:53:54.029747575Z","direction":"IN","type":"RESPONSE","server_id":"github","payload":{"id":1,"jsonrpc":"2.0","result":{"content":[{"text":"{\"number\":58482,\"title\":\"[Provisioning generator] Only generate models reachable from resources (#56733)\",\"body\":\"Fixes #56733.\\n\\nThis PR teaches the provisioning generator to only generate the models and enums actually used by the resources it emits, instead of emitting every input model in the library. It also fixes two related issues that surface once a resource model is shared by multiple resources.\\n\\n## Reachability-based model/enum emission\\n\\n`ProvisioningOutputLibrary` now walks `ArmProviderSchema.Resources` and recursively collects every `InputModelType` / `InputEnumType` reachable through base/derived models, properties, and array/dictionary/union element types. Only those types are passed to the type factory. This drops dead types that are not part of the public Provisioning surface — list-result envelopes (`XxxListResult`), patch wrappers, error models, etc.\\n\\nWhy this matters:\\n- Generated libraries no longer carry irrelevant types (e.g. paging envelopes that have no place in a Provisioning library).\\n- Resolves #56733: the generator used to call `CreateModel` on the resource model from inside dead list-result envelopes, which threw when the same resource model backed multiple resources.\\n\\n## Supporting fixes for shared resource models\\n\\nOnce we stop emitting dead envelopes, the remaining real cases of one input model backing multiple resources (e.g. `ContainerGroupProfile` + `ContainerGroupProfileRevision` via `Azure.ResourceManager.Legacy.LegacyOperations`) need to behave correctly:\\n\\n- **`ProvisioningResourceProvider.BuildName`** — return `ResourceMetadata.ResourceName` so the two providers get distinct file/type names instead of colliding on the input model name.\\n- **`ProvisioningTypeFactory.CreateModelCore`** — when a model is shared by multiple resource providers, prefer the canonical provider whose `ResourceMetadata.ResourceName` matches the model\\u0026#39;s own name. The mgmt `FlattenPropertyVisitor` builds `OutputFlattenPropertyMap` by calling `CreateModel` on the input model, so this routes flattened forwarding properties onto the parent resource (e.g. `Profile`/`ContainerGroupProfile`) and not onto the child revision.\\n\\n## Test\\n\\nAdded `profileRevision.tsp` to the `Provisioning-TypeSpec` test project, mirroring the `ContainerGroupProfile` + `ContainerGroupProfileRevision` pattern: a `ProxyResource` `Profile` plus a virtual sub-resource `ProfileRevision` created via `Azure.ResourceManager.Legacy.LegacyOperations` reusing the same `Profile` model, with `@flattenProperty(Profile.properties)`.\\n\\nGenerated output:\\n- `Profile.cs` and `ProfileRevision.cs` are distinct files.\\n- `Profile` carries the flattened `Description` and `SkuName` properties.\\n- `ProfileRevision` only carries `Id` and `Name` (matches the Bicep-side expectation for revisions).\\n- No dead list-result envelope types are emitted under `Models/`.\\n\\nThis unblocks the `Azure.Provisioning.ContainerInstance` migration (separate PR).\\n\",\"state\":\"closed\",\"html_url\":\"https://github.com/Azure/azure-sdk-for-net/pull/58482\",\"user\":{\"login\":\"ArcturusZhang\",\"id\":10554446,\"profile_url\":\"https://github.com/ArcturusZhang\",\"avatar_url\":\"https://avatars.githubusercontent.com/u/10554446?v=4\"},\"author_association\":\"MEMBER\",\"labels\":[\"CodeGen\",\"Provisioning\"],\"reactions\":{\"total_count\":0,\"+1\":0,\"-1\":0,\"laugh\":0,\"confused\":0,\"heart\":0,\"hooray\":0,\"rocket\":0,\"eyes\":0},\"created_at\":\"2026-04-22T05:59:21Z\",\"updated_at\":\"2026-04-23T05:50:18Z\",\"closed_at\":\"2026-04-23T05:50:13Z\",\"closed_by\":\"ArcturusZhang\"}","type":"text"}]}}}
2026-04-23T05:55:52.8717354Z {"timestamp":"2026-04-23T05:55:39.41406534Z","direction":"OUT","type":"REQUEST","server_id":"safeoutputs","method":"tools/call","payload":{"jsonrpc":"2.0","method":"tools/call","params":{"arguments":{"body":"## Documentation Gap\n\n**Package:** `@azure-typespec/http-client-csharp-provisioning` (generator)\n**Service directory:** `eng/packages/http-client-csharp-provisioning/`\n**Triggered by:** [REDACTED] (PR #58482) by @ArcturusZhang\n\n## What Changed\n\nPR #58482 introduced two significant behavioral changes to the provisioning generator:\n\n1. **Reachability-based model/enum filtering** — `ProvisioningOutputLibrary` now walks resource models' property graphs recursively (`CollectReachableTypes()`) and only passes reachable `InputModelType`/`InputEnumType` instances to the type factory. This replaces the old approach of emitting all input models/enums and relying on the post-processor to prune unreferenced types.\n\n2. **Shared resource model handling** — `ProvisioningTypeFactory.CreateModelCore` now handles the case where one `InputModelType` backs multiple `ProvisioningResourceProvider` instances. It selects the canonical provider whose `ResourceMetadata.ResourceName` matches the model's own name (alphabetically first as a tiebreaker), ensuring flattened properties are forwarded correctly.\n\n## Gaps Found\n\n- **`design.md` § 6 \"Output Library\"** currently says: *\"Only resources are marked as 'types to keep' — the post-processor automatically prunes unreferenced models and enums.\"*  This is no longer accurate. The filtering now happens up-front via `CollectReachableTypes()` in `ProvisioningOutputLibrary`, not via post-processor pruning. The section must be updated to describe the reachability walk (base/derived models, properties, array/dictionary/union element types) and explain why it is needed (avoid dead types like `XxxListResult` paging envelopes, patch wrappers, and error models).\n\n- **`design.md` § 5 \"TypeFactory \u0026 Type Resolution\"** (or § 6 \"Resource Provider\") has no mention of the shared resource model scenario where a single `InputModelType` backs more than one `ProvisioningResourceProvider`. The canonical provider selection logic and why it is necessary (so `FlattenPropertyVisitor` builds `OutputFlattenPropertyMap` on the correct parent resource) is undocumented.\n\n\u003cdetails\u003e\n\u003csummary\u003e\u003cstrong\u003e📐 Implementation Guide\u003c/strong\u003e\u003c/summary\u003e\n\nThis section contains step-by-step instructions for a coding agent to implement the changes described above\n\n### Step 1: Modify files\n\n**File:** `eng/packages/http-client-csharp-provisioning/docs/design.md`\n**Action:** Edit\n\n**Change 1 — Replace the \"Output Library\" paragraph (§ 6)**\n\nLocate this text:\n```\nThe output library bypasses the mgmt output pipeline (which would trigger mgmt-specific type initialization) and instead iterates input models and enums directly, routing each through our TypeFactory. Only resources are marked as \"types to keep\" — the post-processor automatically prunes unreferenced models and enums.\n```\n\nReplace with:\n```\nThe output library bypasses the mgmt output pipeline (which would trigger mgmt-specific type initialization) and instead builds the set of models and enums to emit explicitly via a **reachability walk** (`CollectReachableTypes`). Starting from every resource model registered in `ArmProviderSchema.Resources`, it recursively traverses base/derived types, all property types, and element types of array, dictionary, and union types. Only models and enums reachable through this walk are passed to the type factory.\n\nThis avoids emitting dead types that have no place in a provisioning library — for example, paging envelope types (`XxxListResult`), patch/update wrappers, and generic error models. These types appear in the full input model graph but are never referenced from a resource's property surface.\n```\n\n**Change 2 — Add a \"Shared Resource Models\" subsection**\n\nIn § 5 \"TypeFactory \u0026 Type Resolution\", after the existing \"Resource Detection\" subsection, add:\n\n```markdown\n### Shared Resource Models\n\nIn rare cases the same `InputModelType` backs more than one `ProvisioningResourceProvider` (for example, when `Azure.ResourceManager.Legacy.LegacyOperations` creates a virtual sub-resource whose payload reuses the parent resource's model). `CreateModelCore` must return a single `ModelProvider` for each input model.\n\nWhen the output library records multiple providers for one model, the type factory selects the **canonical provider** — the one whose `ResourceMetadata.ResourceName` matches the model's own name. If no exact match exists, it falls back to the alphabetically first resource name. This ensures the `FlattenPropertyVisitor` attaches flattened forwarding properties to the parent resource (e.g., `Profile` carries the flattened `Description` / `SkuName`), while the sub-resource (e.g., `ProfileRevision`) only exposes the properties it needs.\n```\n\n### Step 2: Add or update code snippets\n\nNo README snippets are required for this change — the affected file is an internal design document, not a user-facing README.\n\n### Step 3: Verify README structure\n\nThe file being updated (`docs/design.md`) is an internal design document, not a user-facing package README. Standard README section validation does not apply.\n\n### Step 4: Validate\n\nThis is a documentation-only change (Markdown). No build or test steps are required. After editing:\n\n1. Confirm the Markdown renders correctly (no broken headings, fenced blocks, or list formatting).\n2. Optionally run a Markdown linter if one is configured for the repo.\n\n\u003c/details\u003e\n\n## Next Steps\n\n\u003e [!TIP]\n\u003e **Ready for automated implementation?** Assign this issue to **@copilot** to have Copilot coding agent implement the changes described in the Implementation Guide above\n","title":"[Provisioning Generator] Docs: Update design.md for reachability-based model filtering and shared resource model handling"},"name":"create_issue"}}}
